report.summary: bankofamerica-secure-login.net is an active Bank of America phishing domain registered November 1, 2024 with full privacy protection via a Panama-registered privacy service. The domain hosts four active credential harvesting pages confirmed by URLhaus and ThreatFox, and resolves exclusively to a bulletproof hosting IP (185.234.219.70). GitHub analysis reveals this domain is linked to an EvilProxy-style phishing kit. This should be blocked immediately.
key_findings: Domain registered 2024-11-01 — 13 days old at time of analysis; newly stood-up phishing infrastructure
URLhaus: 4 active malicious URLs on this domain, all tagged phishing/banking/credential-harvest
ThreatFox: payload_delivery classification at 90% confidence
Resolves to 185.234.219.70 (bulletproof hosting, AS44477 Stark Industries)
GitHub: linked to EvilProxy-style phishing kit by security researchers

mitre_techniques.justification: Domain delivers phishing links impersonating Bank of America to harvest credentials
Typosquat domain registered with privacy protection for disposable phishing use
EvilProxy-style credential capture pages confirmed active on this domain

recommended_actions: Block bankofamerica-secure-login.net at DNS resolver and email gateway immediately
Search email gateway logs for messages containing links to this domain in the past 14 days
Any user who visited this domain should have credentials reset and MFA methods re-enrolled
top_supporting_evidence: URLhaus: 4 active credential phishing pages confirmed online as of 2024-11-14
ThreatFox: payload_delivery classification at 90% confidence from abuse.ch

report.summary: wellsfargo-account-alert.org is an active Wells Fargo phishing domain registered November 2, 2024 — one day after a confirmed companion phishing domain (bankofamerica-secure-login.net). It shares identical nameservers, the same bulletproof hosting IP, and the same Namecheap/Panama privacy registrar. Three active credential-harvesting pages are online. The 24-hour registration gap and shared infrastructure fingerprint with another banking phishing domain strongly indicates a coordinated multi-bank phishing kit.
key_findings: Domain registered 2024-11-02 — 24 hours after companion phishing domain bankofamerica-secure-login.net
Identical nameservers (dns1/2.bullethost-ns.com) and hosting IP (185.234.219.70) as bankofamerica-secure-login.net
URLhaus: 3 active phishing pages confirmed online
ThreatFox: payload_delivery at 85% confidence
Shared phishing database listing with BofA domain confirms same campaign cluster

mitre_techniques.justification: Active Wells Fargo credential phishing pages
Typosquat registered same week as companion banking phishing domains

report.summary: chase-security-verify.com is the third domain in a coordinated three-bank phishing kit registered across three consecutive days (Nov 1–3). It completes a pattern targeting Bank of America, Wells Fargo, and now Chase — the three largest US retail banks — all using identical infrastructure. This is a textbook multi-bank phishing-as-a-service deployment.

repos.name: cert-phishing-feeds/stark-industries-phish
repos.description: Stark Industries AS44477 used as bulletproof phishing hosting — 185.234.219.70 confirmed hosting 6+ banking phish domains

threatfox.summary: ThreatFox: 2 IOC entries (max confidence 88%) — phishing hosting.

report.summary: 185.234.219.70 is a Stark Industries Solutions bulletproof hosting IP that serves as the shared hosting backend for at least six banking phishing domains. Passive DNS reveals the current three-bank campaign (BofA, WF, Chase) plus three earlier targets (Citi, PayPal, USAA), confirming an ongoing multi-institution financial phishing operation. AbuseIPDB rates this IP at 97/100 from 44 reports across 18 reporters. This IP and its surrounding /24 subnet should be blocked across all egress controls.
key_findings: Passive DNS shows 6 banking/financial phishing domains hosted on this single IP across a 60-day period — ongoing systematic operation
AbuseIPDB: 97/100 from 44 reports across 18 distinct reporters
AS44477 (Stark Industries Solutions) is a known bulletproof hosting provider frequently used for phishing and malware hosting
ThreatFox: 2 IOC entries (88% and 85% confidence) confirming payload delivery role
18 OTX pulses from independent researchers corroborating phishing/bulletproof-hosting attribution

mitre_techniques.justification: Bulletproof VPS hosting used to maintain persistent phishing infrastructure across multiple campaigns
HTTPS on port 443 serves phishing pages to victims

recommended_actions: Block 185.234.219.70 and the full /24 subnet 185.234.219.0/24 at perimeter — entire subnet is bulletproof hosting
Search proxy and firewall logs for any connections to this IP in the past 90 days
Hunt for additional phishing domains on this IP using threat intel feeds — at least 6 banking domains confirmed
top_supporting_evidence: Passive DNS: 6 banking phishing domains all pointing to this IP across a 60-day window — confirms systematic hosting role
AbuseIPDB: 97/100 from 44 reports; Stark Industries AS44477 is a well-documented bulletproof hosting provider
top_conflicting_evidence: Shodan shows only standard nginx on 80/443 with no CVEs — no technical fingerprint of malicious hosting beyond DNS attribution

campaign_report.summary: These four indicators form a highly coordinated multi-bank phishing kit targeting the three largest US retail banks (Bank of America, Wells Fargo, Chase) plus a shared bulletproof hosting backend. The three phishing domains were registered on consecutive days (November 1–3, 2024) using identical Namecheap privacy registrations and the same pair of nameservers, and all resolve exclusively to 185.234.219.70 (AS44477 Stark Industries) — a bulletproof hosting provider with a documented history of financial phishing campaigns. Passive DNS on the hosting IP reveals at least three additional earlier banking targets (Citi, PayPal, USAA), confirming this is an ongoing phishing-as-a-service operation rather than a one-off. Campaign confidence is the highest possible: five independent signals (registration timing, shared nameservers, shared IP, shared registrar, cross-listed OTX pulses) all point to a single threat actor.
shared_patterns: All three phishing domains registered via Namecheap with identical Panama privacy service within a 48-hour window (2024-11-01, 2024-11-02, 2024-11-03)
Identical nameservers across all three domains: dns1.bullethost-ns.com and dns2.bullethost-ns.com — strongly suggests automated domain provisioning
All three domains resolve exclusively to 185.234.219.70 (AS44477 Stark Industries) — single shared bulletproof hosting backend
Consistent typosquat naming convention: [bank-name]-[action-verb].{net,org,com} — matches known PhaaS kit templates
OTX cross-listing: two independent research pulses flag all three phishing domains in the same campaign report
URLhaus active malicious URL entries present on all three domains with identical tags: phishing, banking, credential-harvest
campaign_report.threat_actor_hypothesis: The operational pattern — simultaneous multi-bank targeting, automated domain provisioning, bulletproof hosting on AS44477 (Stark Industries), and naming convention consistency — is characteristic of a phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) platform rather than a manual threat actor. The Stark Industries hosting choice and consecutive-day registration cadence have been observed in EvilProxy and Caffeine Kit PhaaS deployments. No named threat group can be attributed with certainty, but the infrastructure and scale suggest a financially motivated criminal operator selling phishing campaigns as a managed service.

mitre_techniques.justification: Three typosquat domains registered simultaneously via the same registrar/privacy service targeting BofA, WF, and Chase
All three delivery domains serve active credential capture pages impersonating major US banks
URLhaus confirms active credential-harvesting pages on all three phishing domains
185.234.219.70 on AS44477 Stark Industries serves as the persistent bulletproof hosting backend for collected credentials
Multi-bank phishing kit designed to harvest banking credentials for financial account takeover

recommended_actions: Block all three phishing domains (bankofamerica-secure-login.net, wellsfargo-account-alert.org, chase-security-verify.com) at DNS resolver, email gateway, and web proxy immediately
Block 185.234.219.70 and the full /24 subnet 185.234.219.0/24 — the entire Stark Industries subnet has a documented phishing hosting history
Search email gateway logs for messages containing links to any of the three phishing domains in the past 30 days; any delivery should trigger user notification and mandatory credential reset
Search web proxy and endpoint DNS logs for user visits to any phishing domain — any confirmed visit requires immediate credential reset + MFA re-enrollment for the affected account

Hunt for additional domains matching the pattern [bank-name]-[action].tld on AS44477 (185.234.0.0/16) — passive DNS reveals at least 3 earlier targets (Citi, PayPal, USAA) suggesting additional active kit domains exist
Share all four indicators with FS-ISAC and your banking sector threat sharing partners for downstream propagation
